today_news.title: OpenAI's Codex can now watch you work once and repeat the task forever
today_news.summary: OpenAI has released the "Record & Replay" feature for its Codex app on macOS: users demonstrate a workflow once, Codex converts it into a reusable "skill," and then repeats it on its own. The feature isn't available yet in the EU, the UK, or Switzerland.

today_news.title: AWS says AI agents lack business context and security, launches two services to patch the gaps
today_news.summary: At its summit in New York, AWS unveiled two new services. Continuum automatically detects, prioritizes, and fixes code vulnerabilities. Context builds a knowledge graph from corporate data to give AI agents the business context they need. Both tackle the same problem: agents that write code fast but...

today_news.title: Sam Altman says a whole generation of researchers held AI back by underestimating what scaling could do
today_news.summary: At a Stanford talk, Sam Altman defended LLM scaling and hit back at skeptics, saying a whole generation of researchers slowed the field by underestimating what scaling could do. He cited OpenAI's recent disproof of a mathematical conjecture as evidence.

today_news.title: The EU doesn't really know what a deepfake is, and that's becoming a problem for retail
today_news.summary: Eurocommerce, the trade association behind Amazon, H&M, and IKEA, wants AI-generated ads exempt from the EU AI Act's transparency rules. The argument: an AI-generated living room image used to sell a sofa isn't a deepfake. Zalando alone says 90 percent of the marketing content on its platform is alr...

today_news.title: NYU finance professor Damodaran warns an AI crash could hit harder than the dot-com bust
today_news.summary: NYU finance professor Aswath Damodaran believes a potential AI crash would be more painful than the bursting of the dot-com bubble because the industry is building massive amounts of debt-financed physical infrastructure rather than lightweight software. Even if AI succeeds, he sees a problem. The a...

today_tech.title: Beyond LoRA: Can you beat the most popular fine-tuning technique?
today_tech.summary: If you want to fine-tune an open model on your own data, you are probably interested in so-called parameter-efficient fine-tuning, in shortPEFT.
